quick_facts.label: Programme format varies by institution
quick_facts.value: BTTM is not delivered through one uniform national format: Jamia Millia Islamia lists a regular four-year, eight-semester BTTM, while Uttarakhand Open University lists a 132-credit BTTM with a 36-month minimum and 72-month maximum duration.

quick_facts.label: Illustrative entry requirement
quick_facts.value: For its 2026–27 BTTM intake, Jamia Millia Islamia required a Senior Secondary/10+2-equivalent qualification with at least 45% in the best five papers.

quick_facts.label: NCHM JEE relationship
quick_facts.value: NCHM JEE should not be treated as a general BTTM entrance route: NCHMCT identifies its JEE-linked undergraduate programme as B.Sc. in Hospitality and Hotel Administration, not BTTM.

quick_facts.label: Work-oriented learning
quick_facts.value: An official JMI BTTM programme handbook identifies preparation for travel-company office roles, tour management, ticketing and event assistance, and includes a 15–18 week on-the-job training component in the eighth semester.

sections.heading: Eligibility
paragraphs: Eligibility is institution-specific rather than nationally fixed. As a current example, Jamia Millia Islamia’s 2026–27 admission schedule for BTTM required a Senior Secondary School Certificate or equivalent from a recognised board and at least 45% aggregate across the best five papers. Applicants should verify the exact board, subject, category, language and minimum-mark rules in the prospectus of the institution they are applying to.
bullets: JMI example for 2026–27: 10+2/equivalent and at least 45% in the best five papers.
Good communication skills and comfort using computers are described as desirable, rather than formal eligibility, in JMI’s BTTM handbook.

sections.heading: Duration and study mode
paragraphs: There is no single duration for every programme carrying the BTTM title. JMI’s regular BTTM is a four-year, eight-semester undergraduate programme. In contrast, Uttarakhand Open University’s BTTM-23 is listed as a 132-credit programme with a minimum duration of 36 months and a maximum duration of 72 months. Compare the awarding university, delivery mode and progression rules—not only the degree title—before applying.
bullets: Regular example: 4 years / 8 semesters at JMI.
Open and distance learning example: 36-month minimum / 72-month maximum at Uttarakhand Open University.

sections.heading: What students study
paragraphs: BTTM curricula combine tourism-domain knowledge with management, operations and employability skills. JMI’s published handbook includes tourism and travel management, tourism resources, management and organisational behaviour, travel-agency management and tour operations, transport services, marketing, tourism geography, digital marketing, aviation and ticketing, sustainable tourism, finance, tour guiding, project work, itinerary costing and preparation, and on-the-job training. Uttarakhand Open University’s current programme similarly includes tourism principles, itinerary preparation and tour packaging, hospitality services, international tourism, tourist transport systems, GDS/air-ticketing and cargo handling, tourism geography, guiding and escorting, adventure tourism, business tourism and a tourism project.
bullets: Operational focus: travel-agency work, tour operations, ticketing, transport, itinerary design and tour costing.
Destination focus: Indian tourism resources, tourism geography, international destinations, heritage and special-interest tourism.
Management focus: marketing, organisational behaviour, finance, accounting, entrepreneurship, communication and digital tools.

Practical exposure can be substantial: JMI specifies 15–18 weeks of eighth-semester industrial exposure/on-the-job training in an approved tourism, travel, hospitality, recreation or leisure-related organisation.

sections.heading: Admission routes
paragraphs: Admission is administered by the university or college offering the BTTM, so applicants should follow that institution’s current prospectus and application portal. For example, JMI’s 2026–27 schedule placed BTTM under programme code B36, with an institutional entrance test scheduled for April 26, 2026 and selection-related steps after the test. This illustrates that BTTM admissions may use institution-specific tests rather than a single all-India BTTM examination.

NCHM JEE is a potentially misleading parent link for this node. NCHMCT states that NCHM JEE is used for admission to its B.Sc. Hospitality and Hotel Administration programme and its affiliated institutes; the official course listing does not present it as an admission test for BTTM. A candidate seeking BTTM should therefore not assume NCHM JEE eligibility or counselling applies unless the target institution expressly says so.
bullets: Start with the offering institution’s current prospectus; entrance test, counselling, interview and document-verification processes can differ.
Treat test dates and deadlines as session-specific. The JMI April 26, 2026 date is an example from the 2026–27 schedule, not a recurring annual deadline.
Confirm whether the programme is regular, distance/open learning, self-financed, or affiliated-college based before paying an application fee.

sections.heading: Fees and additional costs
paragraphs: Fees vary widely by institution, mode and academic session. For dated context, JMI’s 2026–27 prospectus listed BTTM as an eight-semester self-financed programme with a total fee payable per year of ₹32,885. The same prospectus should be checked for the applicable session because fee schedules may be revised.

Open-learning costs can differ substantially. Uttarakhand Open University’s BTTM-23 page lists first-semester admission and examination charges totalling ₹5,100, illustrating why applicants should compare the complete fee structure, examination charges, fieldwork, travel, accommodation and other programme-specific costs rather than relying on a national estimate.
bullets: JMI 2026–27 context: ₹32,885 total fee payable per year for BTTM; this is institution- and session-specific.
Uttarakhand Open University BTTM-23 context: first-semester listed admission fee ₹3,100 plus examination fee ₹2,000, total ₹5,100.
Do not use either figure as a national benchmark; obtain a written, current fee schedule from the institution.

sections.heading: Career and further-study options
paragraphs: The degree aligns directly with operational and supervisory entry paths in tourism and travel businesses. JMI’s programme handbook explicitly identifies travel-company office assistance, tour management, ticketing and event assistance as intended job roles, and describes skills in tour management, ticketing, travel documentation, group handling, customer service, event and conference organisation, basic accounting and itinerary preparation. These support the node’s paths toward travel consulting, ticketing and reservations, tour operations, destination-facing work and travel-agency entrepreneurship; actual job titles and hiring requirements remain employer-specific.

For further study, a tourism-focused master’s degree is one direct route. JMI lists its Master of Tourism and Travel Management as a regular four-semester programme and states that a bachelor’s degree in any discipline with at least 45% aggregate is eligible, subject to its admission process. CAT, CMAT and CUET-PG are examinations or application routes rather than automatic outcomes of BTTM: check the current bulletin and the chosen postgraduate institution’s programme-specific rules. NTA’s CMAT FAQ, for example, states that candidates holding a bachelor’s degree in any discipline, including eligible final-year candidates, may apply for CMAT.
bullets: Career directions: travel consultant; ticketing and reservations executive; tour operations executive; destination-management or tourism-services roles; and travel-agency entrepreneurship.
Further study: MTTM/tourism management, MBA or other management programmes, subject to individual university and examination eligibility.
Entrance routes named in this node: CAT, CMAT and CUET-PG. They should be evaluated against the target programme’s current eligibility, test-paper and admission requirements.
